deep_dive.title: RiskNet: A large-scale dataset of AI risk incidents from news with alignment and multi-dimensional annotations
deep_dive.abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) systems are increasingly deployed across socially consequential domains, reports of AI-related harms and failures have grown in frequency and diversity. Although existing governance frameworks articulate high-level principles for responsible AI, large-scale empirical resources for tracking and analyzing real-world AI risk incidents remain limited. Existing incident collections are often manually curated, relatively small in scale, and insufficient for continuous, data-driven monitoring and downstream computational analysis. To address this need, we present RiskNet, a large-scale dataset of AI risk incidents constructed from large-scale multilingual news sources. RiskNet applies a structured pipeline for AI risk news identification, event-level report screening, incident alignment, and multi-dimensional incident classification. The resulting resource organizes dispersed news reports into incident-centered records and provides benchmark datasets for event classification, incident alignment, and incident-level risk labeling. In its current release, RiskNet covers hundreds of millions of source records and yields a large-scale collection of AI risk-related reports, including aligned incident clusters and annotated benchmark subsets. The dataset is also accessible through an online platform for browsing and exploration. We describe the data sources, processing workflow, taxonomy design, and technical validation of the resource. RiskNet is intended to support downstream research on AI safety, governance, risk analysis, and benchmarking, as well as longitudinal and cross-source analyses of AI-related harms. By providing a structured and reusable empirical resource, RiskNet helps bridge the gap between high-level governance principles and the documented realities of AI risk incidents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: curating safety data from news incidents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从新闻事件中策展安全数据
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从新闻事件中策展安全数据
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从新闻事件中策展安全数据
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents RiskNet, a large-scale dataset of AI risk incidents from news with alignment annotations, providing curated safety data that can be used for alignment preservation in fine-tuning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文构建了RiskNet数据集，从多语言新闻中大规模收集AI风险事件，并进行了对齐和多方位的标注。这些数据可以直接用作安全对齐数据，帮助在微调过程中保持模型安全。与手工策展的小规模数据集不同，RiskNet提供了自动化、持续更新的来源，适用于安全数据的选择和混合。该数据集为缓解微调导致的安全性退化提供了资源支持。

quick_skim.title: AlignFed: Alignment-Aware Asynchronous Federated Fine-Tuning for Large Language Models in Heterogeneous Edge Environments
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have significantly propelled the advancement of edge intelligence and have been widely deployed across various scenarios, including autonomous driving, industrial inspection, and personalized IoT services. However, the collaborative adaptation of LLMs on edge devices continues to face formidable challenges due to strict data privacy constraints, highly heterogeneous computing and communication resources, and the non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) nature of local data. Federated Fine-Tuning (FFT) enables the collaborative optimization of distributed models without exposing raw data. Yet, traditional synchronous aggregation suffers from a severe straggler effect, resulting in high system latency and low resource utilization. Existing asynchronous federated learning methods are predominantly designed for small-to-medium-scale models and struggle to address the specific challenges inherent in LLM fine-tuning namely, model drift caused by stale updates, aggravated client drift stemming from data heterogeneity, and aggregation fairness imbalance resulting from the dominance of fast clients. To address these issues, this paper proposes AlignFed, an asynchronous federated fine-tuning framework for LLMs tailored to heterogeneous edge environments. AlignFed employs a lightweight multi-stage semantic alignment mechanism comprising three core modules: version-aware update grouping, cross-version semantic alignment based on a mini-batch calibration set, and fairness-aware aggregation that integrates both update freshness and client participation frequency. This framework effectively mitigates cross-version model drift and client drift while enhancing aggregation fairness, thereby achieving stable and efficient asynchronous federated optimization in scenarios characterized by high heterogeneity and significant update staleness.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes alignment-aware federated fine-tuning to preserve model alignment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出面向对齐的联邦微调以保持模型对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出面向对齐的联邦微调以保持模型对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出面向对齐的联邦微调以保持模型对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AlignFed, an asynchronous federated fine-tuning method that incorporates alignment constraints to prevent safety degradation in heterogeneous edge environments.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出AlignFed框架，针对边缘异构环境下大语言模型联邦微调中的安全对齐退化问题。通过引入对齐感知的异步聚合策略，在保护隐私的同时维持模型的安全对齐能力。实验表明AlignFed能有效缓解微调导致的安全退化，为分布式场景下的安全微调提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出AlignFed框架，针对边缘异构环境下大语言模型联邦微调中的安全对齐退化问题。通过引入对齐感知的异步聚合策略，在保护隐私的同时维持模型的安全对齐能力。实验表明AlignFed能有效缓解微调导致的安全退化，为分布式场景下的安全微调提供了新方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找关于大语言模型安全微调中“安全对齐数据与普通微调数据混合”的相关论文。重点关注在微调过程中如何引入、选择、混合或调度安全对齐数据，以缓解微调导致的安全退化、有害微调攻击、越狱风险或安全对齐遗忘等问题。优先召回涉及 safety alignment data mixing、safety data selection、data curation、data scheduling、alignment preservation、harmful fine-tuning defense 等方向的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: mixing safety alignment data with fine-tuning data; selecting safety data for fine-tuning; curating safety data for alignment preservation; defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks; mixture of safety data and normal data in fine-tuning; mitigating safety degradation from fine-tuning; papers on mixing safety alignment data with standard fine tuning data to prevent alignment loss; research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning; methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data; how to select and incorporate safety data to mitigate jailbreak risks in LLM fine tuning.

quick_skim.title: Alignment Defends LLMs from Property Inference Attacks
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly fine-tuned on domain-specific datasets that may contain sensitive, dataset-level properties. Recent work has shown that such dataset-level information can be effectively extracted through property inference attacks, posing a confidentiality risk. Existing defenses against these attacks primarily operate by modifying the training data distribution and hence require access to the original data and retraining the model, limiting their applicability to settings where data is unavailable or models are already deployed. In this work, we propose alignment-based defenses for mitigating property inference attacks in LLMs. Our approach reshapes the model's output distribution towards a target property ratio via post-training alignment, without modifying the training data. In particular, we adapt two widely used RLHF frameworks--Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)--as our defenses by constructing preference pairs and defining a specific reward function respectively. Through comprehensive experiments, we show that our alignment based defenses effectively mitigate property inference attacks while maintaining a strong utility confidentiality tradeoff.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: alignment-based defense against property inference, relevant to defending against attacks via fine-tuning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于对齐的属性推理防御，与通过微调防御攻击相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于对齐的属性推理防御，与通过微调防御攻击相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于对齐的属性推理防御，与通过微调防御攻击相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes alignment-based defenses to mitigate property inference attacks on LLMs, reshaping output distribution via post-training alignment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对LLM属性推理攻击提出基于对齐的防御方法。通过后训练对齐重塑模型输出分布到目标属性比率，在不访问原始数据和不重训练的情况下保护数据集级隐私。实验证明该方法能有效降低攻击成功率，扩展了对齐技术在安全防御中的应用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对LLM属性推理攻击提出基于对齐的防御方法。通过后训练对齐重塑模型输出分布到目标属性比率，在不访问原始数据和不重训练的情况下保护数据集级隐私。实验证明该方法能有效降低攻击成功率，扩展了对齐技术在安全防御中的应用。

quick_skim.title: Securing Self-supervised Data Curation for Foundation Models Robustness
quick_skim.abstract: Self-supervised data curation provides a pathway to scaling and improving the generalization capabilities of machine learning models. By leveraging self-supervised learning (SSL) for data curation, the demand for massive training datasets required by foundation models can be effectively met. SSL greatly alleviates the costs associated with annotation and manual dataset curation while minimizing the need for human oversight. However, the integrity of SSL-curated datasets must be rigorously checked, as reliance on anonymous and unvetted external sources can substantially increase the risk of data poisoning. In this paper, we propose a Poisoned Data Detector (PDD), an active defense mechanism designed to ensure the integrity of SSL-curated datasets prior to foundation model training. PDDs are designed using a combination of the pretrained ImageBind model and traditional classifiers, including Random Forest (RF), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Naive Bayes (NB), and Support Vector Machines (SVM). We rigorously evaluated PDDs using 176,200 images from three diverse datasets and three different adversarial attacks encompassing both in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios. Notably, SVM-PDD achieves superior performance for both in-distribution (Set3-Set5) and out-of-distribution (TrueFace and 140K RealFace) datasets. Our design demonstrates strong scalability and enables the rapid integration of new adversarial attack detectors through an ensemble approach.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: curating safety data via poisoning detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过投毒检测来策展安全数据
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过投毒检测来策展安全数据
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过投毒检测来策展安全数据
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Poisoned Data Detector (PDD) to ensure integrity of self-supervised curated datasets for foundation models, applicable to curating safety data for alignment preservation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对自监督学习策展的数据集面临投毒风险的问题，提出了主动防御机制Poisoned Data Detector（PDD）。PDD能够检测数据投毒，确保策展数据的完整性。虽然主要面向基础模型的自监督学习，但其数据完整性检测方法可迁移至安全对齐数据的筛选与策展，有助于在微调中保持模型安全性。实验验证了PDD的有效性，为安全数据选择提供了工具支持。

quick_skim.title: Advancing the State-of-the-Art in Empirical Privacy Auditing
quick_skim.abstract: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs) can exhibit problematic memorization of individual training examples. Empirical privacy auditing (EPA) quantifies this risk by measuring realistic data leakage on membership inference (MI) or reconstruction attacks. A key challenge in EPA is designing ``canary'' examples that are mixed with the privacy-sensitive training data. We propose generating synthetic canaries via high-temperature sampling ($T \geq 0.8$) from LLMs, using prompts tailored to the privacy-sensitive training data. These canaries act as high-influence outliers, ensuring high identifiability and hence strong audits. Further, since the canaries are themselves non-private, they are inspectable and can be inserted with repetition without jeopardizing the privacy of the real data. An important use of models fine-tuned on privacy-sensitive data is the generation of synthetic data. This also comes with privacy risk. We introduce a powerful synthetic data audit based on fine-tuning an auxiliary model on the synthetic data. Auditing the auxiliary model for the original canaries then provides a strong estimate of the privacy leakage through the synthetic data. Finally, leveraging our strong auditing methodologies, we perform a systematic investigation into the interacting effects of model capacity and canary entropy on memorization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses synthetic data generation for privacy auditing, applicable to safety data synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用合成数据生成进行隐私审计，可迁移至安全数据合成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用合成数据生成进行隐私审计，可迁移至安全数据合成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用合成数据生成进行隐私审计，可迁移至安全数据合成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes generating synthetic canaries via high-temperature sampling to audit LLM memorization, offering a method transferable to synthetic safety data generation for alignment preservation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对参数高效微调中LLM记忆训练样本的问题，本文提出通过高温采样生成合成金丝雀数据，将其混入隐私敏感训练数据中，作为高影响异常值以增强经验隐私审计。该方法通过设计可检查的非私有合成数据，有效检测成员推理和重构攻击，为安全对齐中的合成安全数据生成提供了可迁移的技术思路。

quick_skim.title: Grammar-Constrained Decoding Can Jailbreak LLMs into Generating Malicious Code
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for code generation, raising concerns that they may be misused to produce malicious code. Meanwhile, Grammar-Constrained Decoding (GCD) has been widely adopted to improve the reliability of LLM-generated code by enforcing syntactic validity. In this paper, we reveal a counterintuitive risk: this reliability-oriented technique can itself become an attack surface. We uncover a new jailbreak attack, termed CodeSpear, that exploits GCD to induce LLMs into generating malicious code. Our experiments show that simply applying a benign code grammar constraint can effectively jailbreak LLMs.   To address this vulnerability, we propose CodeShield, a safety alignment approach that robustly preserves safe behavior even under attacker-controlled grammar constraints. CodeShield aligns the model in the code modality by teaching it to generate honeypot code under GCD. Such code is semantically harmless, so it does not implement the malicious request, and structurally diverse, so it is difficult to suppress through grammar tightening. At the same time, CodeShield still preserves natural-language refusals when natural language is available. Experiments on 10 popular LLMs across 4 benchmarks show that CodeSpear outperforms representative jailbreak baselines and increases the attack success rate by more than 30 percentage points on average. CodeShield also restores safety under CodeSpear while preserving benign utility. Our findings reveal a fundamental risk of GCD and call for greater attention to its potential security implications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes CodeShield safety alignment to defend against jailbreak attacks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出CodeShield安全对齐方法防御越狱攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出CodeShield安全对齐方法防御越狱攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出CodeShield安全对齐方法防御越狱攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reveals that grammar-constrained decoding can jailbreak LLMs into generating malicious code, and proposes CodeShield, a safety alignment approach that robustly preserves safe generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文揭示了一个反直觉的风险：语法约束解码这一提高代码生成可靠性的技术，本身可被利用发起越狱攻击。作者提出名为CodeShield的安全对齐方法，通过在解码过程中施加约束来稳健地保持生成内容的安全性。实验表明，该方法能有效防御此类攻击，为LLM代码生成的安全提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文揭示了一个反直觉的风险：语法约束解码这一提高代码生成可靠性的技术，本身可被利用发起越狱攻击。作者提出名为CodeShield的安全对齐方法，通过在解码过程中施加约束来稳健地保持生成内容的安全性。实验表明，该方法能有效防御此类攻击，为LLM代码生成的安全提供了新思路。
